title: Fix profile setup flow for pre-existing users

requirements.summary: Check for existing profile before showing ProfileSetupModal and skip modal for pre-existing users
acceptanceCriteria: When a pre-existing user logs in, the ProfileSetupModal is NOT shown.
The app detects the existing profile and proceeds directly to the dashboard.
No silent failure or unhandled '!User already exists' error occurs for pre-existing users.
New users (no existing profile) still see the ProfileSetupModal as before.

file_operations.description: Update the profile existence check logic to use doesCallerUserProfileExist() instead of checking if userProfile is null. Change the showProfileSetup condition from 'isAuthenticated && !profileLoading && isFetched && userProfile === null' to 'isAuthenticated && !profileLoading && profileExists === false', ensuring the modal only appears when the backend confirms no profile exists. This prevents showing the modal to users who already have profiles.

Add a new query hook useDoesCallerUserProfileExist that calls actor.doesCallerUserProfileExist() and returns a boolean indicating profile existence. This query should be enabled when the actor is available and not fetching, with retry disabled. Use the same loading state pattern as useGetCallerUserProfile to properly reflect actor dependency and prevent flash states.

Update error handling in the registerUser mutation's onError handler to catch and display a user-friendly message when the backend returns 'User already exists' or similar registration errors. Show a toast notification explaining that the user already has a profile and redirect them to the dashboard instead of silently failing.

requirements.summary: Ensure only administrators can create profiles for existing users and prevent regular users from re-registering
acceptanceCriteria: Admin users can still create or update profiles for existing users via the AdminPanel.
Non-admin users cannot trigger a new profile registration if a profile already exists for their principal.
Attempting to register an already-existing user as a non-admin results in an appropriate user-facing message (e.g., 'You already have a profile') rather than a silent failure.

file_operations.description: Add a guard in the handleSubmit function that checks profile existence using the new useDoesCallerUserProfileExist hook before allowing registration submission. If a profile already exists and the user attempts to register, show an error toast with the message 'You already have a profile' and close the modal, preventing duplicate registration attempts by non-admin users.

Verify that the AdminPanel retains its ability to call saveUserProfileAsAdmin for existing users without restrictions. Ensure admin users can update participation levels and profiles for any user regardless of existing profile status. No changes to conditional rendering logic or admin-only guards are needed; this operation confirms that admin profile management capabilities remain unrestricted.
